studyName: Investigating Genetics in Suspected Congenital Syndromes

tables.description: This subject phenotypes data table reports each subject's age at time of electronic health records search, self-reported sex, self-reported race, genotype status for: JAK1 R506C, JAK1 S700N, JAK1 V985I, status for autoimmunity, status for atopy, status for colitis, and status for dermatitis.

variables.name: SUBJECT_AGE
variables.description: Subject age at time of electronic health record search

variables.name: SUBJECT_ATOPY
variables.description: Subject electronic health record contains atopy phenotype

variables.name: SUBJECT_AUTOIMMUNITY
variables.description: Subject electronic health record contains autoimmunity phenotype

variables.name: SUBJECT_COLITIS
variables.description: Subject electronic health record contains colitis phenotype

variables.name: SUBJECT_DERMATITIS
variables.description: Subject electronic health record contains dermatitis phenotype

variables.name: SUBJECT_JAK1_R506C
variables.description: Subject  genotype encodes JAK1 R506C amino acid change

variables.name: SUBJECT_JAK1_S700N
variables.description: Subject  genotype encodes JAK1 S700N amino acid change

variables.name: SUBJECT_JAK1_V985I
variables.description: Subject  genotype encodes JAK1 V985I amino acid change

tables.description: This sample attributes data table reports each sample's body site of origin, analyte type, and tumor status.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

tables.description: This subject consent data table reports each subject's subject ID, consent, and biological sex.

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study subject IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs.
